title: Error: writing blob: storing blob to file: unexpected EOF
body:
### Issue Description

This is in continuation to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12962, https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17545. The issue still persists even after upgrading podman client to 4.4.4 and podman server to 4.4.2 on macOS Ventura (13.2.1) on M1 Max.

**Client Logs**
```sh
➜  ~ podman --version
podman version 4.4.4
➜  ~ podman pull --platform linux/amd64 hub.foobar.com/apps/nginx:1.0.3_RELEASE_20230308210704612
Trying to pull hub.foobar.com/apps/nginx:1.0.3_RELEASE_20230308210704612...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:3d51a39ba405fc1b46007633c09b5388246bde32fdc9e224fd1d46e15f8cab4f
Copying blob sha256:391aa60d798921957837d6d49b2c8ec1d40090acdb93e7b63e2b10311de10bb1
Copying blob sha256:7608715873ec5c02d370e963aa9b19a149023ce218887221d93fe671b3abbf58
Copying blob sha256:efd2628a9d8be4265cd14a2270cb994333917832af3dab6ba06f14b3f8ea673f
Copying blob sha256:6d87af36c87a2afe3797ea76c0f29e630ce254a41e3183e7c75da10c3afd73ea
Copying blob sha256:18e855ea6c2e5aea18b9594ecf1324124c242798b9cf7bd33a3c0175d0ee1aec
Copying blob sha256:b8bade255407e5aae315a50508ee20045f1bf60870d23a8f2171ffcaacbe3604
Copying blob sha256:580263e2d3156c888372e0695254fcf0c80518e6efcacc524e70afe323928f6e
Copying blob sha256:6068cad611ea0100a9f89066da5246eade076e24a12c48989b51ed63fba8ec22
Copying blob sha256:3cfc1b709af2ad4de3724b037345ef34da299623103202f9d30f7373271b851e
Error: writing blob: storing blob to file "/var/tmp/storage3331955061/6": happened during read: (heuristic tuning data: last retry 56506494, current offset 56571510; 258707.443 ms total, 252.803 ms since progress): unexpected EOF
➜  ~
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Same as: 
2. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12962 
3. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17545

### podman info output

```yaml
host:
  arch: arm64
  buildahVersion: 1.29.0
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpu
  - io
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
```

comments.body:
Look at all the log lines with `decision inputs`: that’s quite a few “unexpected EOF” errors. We do retry failed requests, but the retries do need to stop _eventually_. There’s probably no universally-right threshold for that, but this does seem rather far in direction of “too many”, especially in how they seem to come in bursts. (For retrying, we require either some time between retries, or some progress being made; here we see failures within a few seconds, and at around 60 kB of data.)

In this case, the copy has failed for _three_ separate reasons!

So I’d rather suggest that this might be best improved by understanding the root cause of the failures, instead of papering them over with retries. Especially it’s rather suggestive that two blobs fail with exactly the same 65079 offset, and one other failure is at offset 56571510-56506494=65016 . That seems very non-random (TCP slow start might be involved, but still…); wildly guessing, is there something in the network limiting the number of concurrent connections? Some kind of connection-tracking firewall with full state tables? I don’t have any expertise in this area or in debugging that…

---

Focusing only on the heuristics, that was further relaxed in https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1847 / https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1859 , which is not yet included in Podman 4.4.2. That might have made a difference for two of the failures, but not for the last one (were a retried connection also fails within about 65 kB ).

@mtrmac I too can't guess the root cause but I can tell for sure that pulling the same image or any other image on the same network fails everytime with Podman but works successfully with Docker CLI (Docker Desktop) every single time on the same network.
FWIW, I ran into this today when using the SHA URL.  If I use a URL like `ghcr.io/pccowboy/postscript_test_project:main`, my pull succeeds.  HTH
@dpnishant @pccowboy are you running behind a proxy?  (misconfigured) Proxies have turned out be causing quite a lot of unexpected EOFs in the past.

@vrothberg Not proxy but behind corporate VPN gateway and CASB. I reported the same issue to the folks from my corporate vpn gateway and the issue seems to have gone away for now, by enabling "Always connect using SSL" in the VPN client.

Thanks, @dpnishant.

I think we can close the issue then.
